commands.review_summary:
The PR changes video base64 limit resolution to reuse the shared base64-size estimator and adds a focused unit test for boundary rounding and the global cap.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +28. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main uses floor-rounded expansion for the configured cap while the caller uses ceil-rounded base64 sizing for the actual video payload. A raw size where `maxBytes % 3 !== 0` can be within the raw cap but exceed the incorrectly rounded base64 cap.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No automated repair is needed; maintainer action is normal review/merge handling after required checks are green.

target.title: fix(media-understanding): align video base64 byte limits
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Video max byte limits are compared after estimating the inline base64 payload size, but `resolveVideoMaxBase64Bytes()` converted the configured raw byte limit with `Math.floor(maxBytes * (4 / 3))`. That underestimates any raw byte limit that is not divisible by 3, so a video exactly within the configured raw byte cap can be rejected before provider submission.

For example, `maxBytes = 1` expands to a 4-byte base64 payload, but the previous resolved cap was 1.

## Why This Change Was Made

The helper already has the correct base64 sizing primitive, `estimateBase64Size(bytes)`, which rounds byte counts to complete base64 quanta with `Math.ceil(bytes / 3) * 4`. This change reuses that primitive for the configured max byte limit, keeping the comparison symmetric with the per-file estimate while preserving the shared `DEFAULT_VIDEO_MAX_BASE64_BYTES` cap.

A focused regression test covers the non-multiple-of-three boundaries that previously failed.

## User Impact

Users with custom video byte limits no longer see valid videos rejected only because the expanded base64 limit was rounded down. The global payload cap still applies, so this does not relax the existing maximum request size guard.

## Evidence

Direct boundary probe:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./packages/media-understanding-common/src/video.ts').then(({ resolveVideoMaxBase64Bytes }) => console.log(JSON.stringify([1, 2, 3, 4].map((maxBytes) => ({ maxBytes, maxBase64Bytes: resolveVideoMaxBase64Bytes(maxBytes) })))))"
[{"maxBytes":1,"maxBase64Bytes":4},{"maxBytes":2,"maxBase64Bytes":4},{"maxBytes":3,"maxBase64Bytes":4},{"maxBytes":4,"maxBase64Bytes":8}]
```

Targeted unit test:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts packages/media-understanding-common/src/video.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 C:/Users/lin20/Documents/New project 3/openclaw


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
   Start at  21:04:15
   Duration  967ms (transform 682ms, setup 515ms, import 16ms, tests 145ms, environment 0ms)
```

Formatting:

```text
$ node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check packages/media-understanding-common/src/video.ts packages/media-understanding-common/src/video.test.ts
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 1235ms on 2 files using 32 threads.
```

Whitespace:

```text
$ git diff --check
```
